title: 4.9 Huffman Trees
subtitle: Optimal binary trees, coding applications, and expanded follow-up topics

content.introduction: Huffman trees produce prefix-free variable-length codes that minimize expected encoding length for known symbol frequencies.

sections.title: 4.9.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define weighted path length, prefix-free coding, and optimality objective in Huffman construction.
examples: Compute weighted path length for a simple code tree
Explain why no codeword is prefix of another

sections.title: 4.9.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe greedy construction with a min-priority queue by repeatedly merging two least-frequency nodes.
examples: Generate code table by left=0/right=1 traversal
Decode a bitstream with tree navigation

sections.title: 4.9.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Discuss coding efficiency, decoding via tree traversal, and practical constraints such as symbol table transmission.
examples: Estimate compression gain from skewed frequencies
Compare static vs adaptive coding choices
Use Huffman in text and media compression pipelines

keyTakeaways: Construct Huffman trees with greedy merges
Generate and use prefix-free code tables
Apply Huffman ideas in practical codecs
